 {"id":"caam-1xv","title":"Research and implement Claude setup-token for headless auth","description":"# Research and Implement Claude Headless Auth

## Research Findings (Completed)

### Available Methods

#### 1. setup-token + CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN (Primary)
```bash
# Generate token
claude setup-token
# Returns: your-oauth-token-here

# Use in headless environment  
export CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN="your-oauth-token-here"
claude -p "your prompt"
```

**Known Issue**: GitHub #8938 reports the token doesn't fully bypass the startup interactive flow. User still sees theme/auth prompts even with token set.

#### 2. ANTHROPIC_API_KEY (API Key Mode)
```bash
export ANTHROPIC_API_KEY="sk-ant-..."
claude -p "your prompt"
```
Works for print mode (-p), may not work for interactive sessions.

#### 3. Credential File Transfer (Workaround)
Transfer ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json to remote machine:
```bash
scp ~/.config/claude-code/auth.json user@server:~/.config/claude-code/auth.json
```

#### 4. SSH Port Forwarding (Workaround)
```bash
# Local machine
ssh -L 8080:localhost:8080 user@remote-server
# On remote, run claude /login, open the localhost URL locally
```

### Current Status in Claude Code

- No native device code flow (like codex --device-auth)
- setup-token exists but has bugs (#8938)
- Credential transfer is the most reliable workaround
- GitHub #7100 requests proper headless auth documentation

### Implementation Path for caam

1. **AuthModeSetupToken**: New auth mode using CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN
2. **Token Import**: Command to import setup-token result
3. **Credential Export/Import**: Enhanced backup/restore for headless transfer

### Files to Modify

- internal/provider/claude/claude.go
- Add setupToken flow that:
  a. Checks for CLAUDE_CODE_OAUTH_TOKEN env var
  b. If not set, prompts user to run \`claude setup-token\` elsewhere
  c. Stores token in profile for future use

### Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Document current limitations clearly
- [ ] Implement token-based auth if feasible
- [ ] Fallback: Provide clear instructions for credential transfer via caam backup/restore

### References

- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/8938
- https://github.com/anthropics/claude-code/issues/7100
- https://claude-did-this.com/claude-hub/getting-started/setup-container-guide","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T17:37:22.089377565-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T18:06:10.248176035-05:00","close_reason":"Research complete. Claude doesn't have reliable device-code. setup-token has bugs (GitHub #8938). Recommendation: Use vault transfer approach (caam export/import) which works universally."}
 {"id":"caam-234","title":"Usage Analytics","description":"# Usage Analytics

## Purpose
Track profile usage activity to give users visibility into their patterns and help identify underutilized or overutilized profiles.

## Background (from codex-pool)
codex-pool tracks detailed usage with BoltDB:
- Per-request logs (debugging)
- Per-account aggregates (dashboard)
- Automatic pruning (retention policy)

## Adaptation for caam
caam does not proxy requests, so we cannot track API usage directly. Instead, we track **caam activity**:
- Profile activations
- Login/refresh events
- Errors encountered
- Session durations

## What This Epic Delivers
1. SQLite database setup and schema
2. Activity logging API
3. Session duration tracking
4. Usage aggregation queries
5. CLI usage command
6. TUI usage panel (optional)

## Why SQLite (not BoltDB)
- Query flexibility (complex aggregations)
- Familiar tooling (sqlite3 CLI, DB browsers)
- Mature ecosystem
- CGO-free drivers available (modernc.org/sqlite)

## Database Schema
```sql
CREATE TABLE activity_log (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    timestamp DATETIME NOT NULL,
    event_type TEXT NOT NULL,
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    details TEXT,
    duration_seconds INTEGER
);

CREATE TABLE profile_stats (
    provider TEXT NOT NULL,
    profile_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    total_activations INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_errors INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    total_active_seconds INTEGER DEFAULT 0,
    last_activated DATETIME,
    last_error DATETIME,
    PRIMARY KEY (provider, profile_name)
);
```

## Event Types
- `activate` - Profile activated
- `deactivate` - Profile deactivated (implicit when switching)
- `login` - User logged in
- `refresh` - Token refreshed
- `error` - Error occurred
- `switch` - Switched from one profile to another

## Retention Policy
- Keep detailed logs for 30 days (configurable)
- Keep aggregates indefinitely
- Auto-prune on startup

## User Experience
```bash
$ caam usage
Profile Usage (last 7 days)
───────────────────────────
claude/work         42 sessions   18.5 hrs
claude/personal     12 sessions    4.2 hrs
codex/main          28 sessions   12.1 hrs

$ caam usage --profile claude/work
Session History for claude/work (last 7 days)
─────────────────────────────────────────────
2025-12-17 09:15  2.3 hrs  (active)
2025-12-16 14:20  1.8 hrs
2025-12-16 09:00  3.1 hrs
```

## Dependencies
- None (parallel to health features)

## Future Enhancements
- Export to CSV/JSON
- Integration with provider usage APIs
- Cost tracking (if providers expose this)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:16:31.226242657-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:44:47.338639408-05:00","close_reason":"Fully implemented: SQLite db package (db.go, migrations.go), activity logging (events.go), session tracking (session.go), usage CLI command with table/json/csv output. All tests pass.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-234","depends_on_id":"caam-3am","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:26:48.554681767-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
 {"id":"caam-2bm","title":"Multi-Machine Vault Sync","description":"# Epic: Multi-Machine Vault Sync

## Overview
Enable intelligent synchronization of vault data across multiple machines, automatically pushing fresh auth tokens to all machines in a sync pool.

## Background
Power users have multiple machines (work laptop, home desktop, cloud VM). When a token is refreshed on one machine, they want it available on all machines without manual copying. This is the ultimate "set and forget" workflow.

## User Story
As a user with multiple machines, I want my auth tokens to automatically sync across all machines, so that I always have the freshest credentials available everywhere.

## Design Philosophy
- **Push-based, not polling**: When auth is refreshed/backed up, immediately push to other machines
- **Freshness wins**: Always prefer the token with the latest expiry time
- **Union merge**: Profiles are merged across machines (A has alice+bob, B has alice+carol → both get alice+bob+carol)
- **Graceful degradation**: Offline machines get synced when they come back online
- **SSH transport**: Leverage existing SSH infrastructure for secure, authenticated transport

## Machine Discovery

### 1. SSH Config Parsing
Parse `~/.ssh/config` to auto-discover hosts:
```
Host work-laptop
    HostName 192.168.1.100
    User jeff
    IdentityFile ~/.ssh/work_key
```

### 2. User CSV File
Create/maintain `~/caam_sync_list.csv`:
```csv
machine_name,ip_address,path_to_ssh_key_file
work-laptop,192.168.1.100,~/.ssh/id_ed25519
home-desktop,10.0.0.50,~/.ssh/home_key
cloud-vm,34.123.45.67,~/.ssh/cloud_rsa
```

### 3. TUI Management
- View sync pool in TUI
- Add/edit/remove machines
- Test connectivity
- View sync status per machine

## Sync Protocol

### Freshness Determination
OAuth tokens contain expiry information. Compare:
1. `expires_at` / `expires_in` from token JSON
2. File modification time as tiebreaker
3. Deterministic: later expiry always wins

### Sync Algorithm
```
On local auth change (backup/refresh/activate):
  1. For each machine in sync pool:
     a. SSH connect (with retry)
     b. Read remote token for same provider/profile
     c. Compare freshness (local vs remote)
     d. If local newer → push to remote
     e. If remote newer → pull to local (unexpected but handle it)
     f. Log sync action
  2. Queue failed machines for retry
  3. Update last_sync timestamp
```

### Triggering
- **Auto-trigger**: After backup, refresh, or activate (if sync enabled)
- **Manual**: `caam sync` command
- **Periodic retry**: Process queue on any caam command

## CLI Commands
```bash
caam sync                     # Sync now with all machines
caam sync status              # Show sync pool status
caam sync add <machine>       # Add machine to pool
caam sync remove <machine>    # Remove machine from pool
caam sync test <machine>      # Test SSH connectivity
caam sync enable              # Enable auto-sync
caam sync disable             # Disable auto-sync
caam sync log                 # Show sync history
```

## TUI Integration
- New panel/view for sync status
- Machine list with status indicators (🟢 synced, 🟡 pending, 🔴 error)
- Add/remove/edit machines
- Manual sync button
- Last sync timestamp per machine

## CSV File Auto-Creation
On first sync-related command, if `~/caam_sync_list.csv` doesn't exist:
1. Create file with header row
2. Optionally pre-populate from ~/.ssh/config
3. Print helpful message about editing the file

## Sync State Storage
`~/.local/share/caam/sync/`
```
sync/
├── pool.json           # Machine definitions and status
├── queue.json          # Pending syncs (for retry)
├── history.json        # Sync log (last N syncs)
└── state/              # Per-machine state
    ├── work-laptop.json
    └── home-desktop.json
```

## Edge Cases

### Machine Offline
- Queue the sync operation
- Retry on next caam command or sync
- Mark machine as "pending" in status

### Conflicting Updates
- Rare: same profile refreshed on two machines simultaneously
- Resolution: compare expires_at, later wins
- If exactly equal: prefer local (no network needed)

### New Machine Joins
- Full sync on first connect
- Pull all profiles from existing pool
- Push all local profiles

### Machine Removed
- Just remove from pool
- No data deletion on remote or local

### First Sync Ever
- If pool is empty, nothing to do
- If pool has machines, do full bidirectional sync

## Security Considerations
- All transport over SSH (encrypted)
- SSH keys must be properly secured
- No password storage - uses existing SSH key auth
- Warn user if CSV file has loose permissions
- Consider: sign sync data to prevent tampering?

## Requirements
- SSH client installed (standard on macOS/Linux)
- SSH key authentication configured
- Network access between machines (may need VPN for remote)
- Same caam version on all machines (or compatible)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Parse ~/.ssh/config for host discovery
- [ ] Create/read ~/caam_sync_list.csv
- [ ] SSH connectivity test per machine
- [ ] Freshness comparison using token expiry
- [ ] Push newer tokens to remote machines
- [ ] Pull newer tokens from remote machines
- [ ] Queue and retry for offline machines
- [ ] Auto-sync on backup/refresh (when enabled)
- [ ] Manual sync command
- [ ] TUI sync status view
- [ ] TUI machine management
- [ ] Sync history logging

## Dependencies
- Requires Export/Import Bundle epic for bundle format knowledge (shared serialization)

## Effort Estimate
Large - new subsystem with networking, state management, error handling

## Future Enhancements (Out of Scope)
- Cloud-based sync (Dropbox, Google Drive integration)
- Web-based sync dashboard
- Mobile notification when tokens sync
- Conflict resolution UI","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-19T14:38:54.734422634-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-19T17:12:26.143215749-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-19T17:12:26.143215749-05:00","close_reason":"Multi-Machine Vault Sync epic complete. All components implemented: sync infrastructure, CLI commands, auto-sync, and TUI panel."}
-{"id":"caam-39a","title":"Add sync management to TUI","description":"# Task: Add sync management to TUI

## Context
Expose sync functionality in the TUI for users who prefer the graphical interface. This includes viewing sync status, managing machines, and triggering syncs.

## TUI Changes

### New Panel: Sync Status
Add a new panel/view accessible via 'S' key:

```
┌─ Sync Pool ─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│ Status: Enabled (auto-sync on)                              │
│ Last sync: 5 minutes ago                                    │
│                                                             │
│ Machines:                                                   │
│   🟢 work-laptop     192.168.1.100     synced 5m ago       │
│   🟢 home-desktop    10.0.0.50         synced 5m ago       │
│   🔴 cloud-vm        34.123.45.67      offline (2h)        │
│                                                             │
│ Pending: 0 | Failed: 1 | Queue: 1 item                     │
│                                                             │
│ [a]dd  [r]emove  [t]est  [s]ync now  [e]dit CSV  [q]uit   │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Status Indicators
- 🟢 Online, synced
- 🟡 Online, pending sync
- 🔴 Offline or error
- 🔄 Currently syncing

### Keyboard Shortcuts (in sync view)
- 'a' - Add machine (opens dialog)
- 'r' - Remove selected machine (with confirmation)
- 't' - Test selected machine connectivity
- 's' - Sync now with all/selected machine
- 'e' - Edit CSV file in editor
- 'l' - View sync log
- 'Enter' - View machine details
- 'Esc/q' - Return to main view

### Add Machine Dialog
When 'a' is pressed:
```
┌─ Add Machine to Sync Pool ─────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ Name:     [work-laptop________________]                     │
│ Address:  [192.168.1.100______________]                     │
│ SSH Key:  [~/.ssh/id_ed25519__________]                     │
│                                                             │
│           [Add] [Test & Add] [Cancel]                       │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Machine Details View
When Enter is pressed on a machine:
```
┌─ Machine: work-laptop ──────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ Address:      192.168.1.100                                 │
│ SSH User:     jeff                                          │
│ SSH Key:      ~/.ssh/id_ed25519                             │
│ Remote Path:  ~/.local/share/caam                           │
│ Status:       Online (latency: 23ms)                        │
│ Added:        2025-12-15 10:30:00                           │
│ Last Sync:    2025-12-19 14:24:00 (5 min ago)              │
│                                                             │
│ Profiles synced: 8                                          │
│   claude: 5 profiles                                        │
│   codex: 2 profiles                                         │
│   gemini: 1 profile                                         │
│                                                             │
│ Recent sync activity:                                       │
│   14:24:00  claude/alice@gmail.com    pushed    ✓          │
│   14:24:01  codex/work@company.com    pulled    ✓          │
│                                                             │
│           [Test] [Sync] [Remove] [Back]                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Sync Progress Overlay
When sync is running:
```
┌─ Syncing... ────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                                                             │
│ work-laptop:   [████████████████░░░░] 80%                  │
│   ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com (pushed)                        │
│   ✓ claude/bob@gmail.com (up to date)                      │
│   → codex/work@company.com (syncing...)                    │
│                                                             │
│ home-desktop:  [████████░░░░░░░░░░░░] 40%                  │
│   ✓ claude/alice@gmail.com (pushed)                        │
│   ...                                                       │
│                                                             │
│                        [Cancel]                             │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
```

### Integration with Main View
In the main profile list, show sync status:
```
claude
  alice@gmail.com     active    🔄 syncing
  bob@gmail.com                 🟢 synced
  carol@gmail.com               ⚠️ pending
```

### Help Panel Update
Add sync shortcuts to help:
```
Sync:
  S       Open sync panel
  Ctrl+S  Sync now (from any view)
```

## Implementation

### New Files
- `internal/tui/sync_view.go` - Sync panel view
- `internal/tui/sync_model.go` - Sync panel model
- `internal/tui/sync_update.go` - Sync panel update handlers

### Model Changes
```go
type Model struct {
    // ... existing fields ...
    
    syncView    *SyncViewModel
    showingSync bool
}

type SyncViewModel struct {
    pool          *sync.SyncPool
    machines      []*sync.Machine
    selectedIdx   int
    syncing       bool
    syncProgress  map[string]float64
    lastRefresh   time.Time
}
```

### Key Handling
In `update.go`:
```go
case "S":
    return m.showSyncView()
    
case "ctrl+s":
    return m.triggerSyncNow()
```

### Sync Panel
```go
func (m SyncViewModel) View() string {
    // Render sync panel using lipgloss
    // - Header with status
    // - Machine list with status indicators
    // - Footer with keybindings
}

func (m SyncViewModel) Update(msg tea.Msg) (SyncViewModel, tea.Cmd) {
    switch msg := msg.(type) {
    case tea.KeyMsg:
        switch msg.String() {
        case "a":
            return m.showAddMachineDialog()
        case "r":
            return m.confirmRemoveMachine()
        // etc
        }
    case SyncProgressMsg:
        // Update progress display
    case SyncCompleteMsg:
        // Show results, refresh
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 'S' key opens sync panel
- [ ] Sync panel shows machine list with status
- [ ] Add machine dialog works
- [ ] Remove machine with confirmation works
- [ ] Test connectivity from TUI works
- [ ] Sync now from TUI works
- [ ] Sync progress overlay shows during sync
- [ ] Machine details view shows full info
- [ ] Sync log accessible from TUI
- [ ] Edit CSV opens editor
- [ ] Help text updated
- [ ] Ctrl+S triggers sync from any view
- [ ] Proper error handling and display

## Files to Create
- `internal/tui/sync_view.go`
- `internal/tui/sync_model.go`
- `internal/tui/sync_update.go`

## Files to Modify
- `internal/tui/model.go` - Add sync state
- `internal/tui/update.go` - Add key handlers
- `internal/tui/view.go` - Add sync panel rendering
- `internal/tui/help.go` - Update help text

## Dependencies
- Depends on: caam-i5i (text input dialog - for add machine)
- Depends on: caam-3wx (sync infrastructure)
- Depends on: caam-8y9 (CLI sync commands - for consistency)

 {"id":"caam-3am","title":"Smart Profile Management (codex-pool inspired)","description":"# Smart Profile Management Epic

## Origin & Inspiration
This epic brings intelligence from [codex-pool](https://github.com/darvell/codex-pool) to caam. codex-pool is a sophisticated proxy that load-balances requests across multiple AI accounts with automatic failover, token refresh, and usage tracking.

**Key Insight**: While codex-pool answers "which account for THIS request?", caam answers "which account for my WORK SESSION?" The intelligence transfers, but the application differs.

## Goals
1. **Prevent auth failures** through proactive token refresh
2. **Surface profile health** so users can make informed choices
3. **Track activity** for visibility into usage patterns
4. **Reduce friction** with hot reload and project associations

## Non-Goals (for now)
- Proxy mode (request interception)
- Automatic profile switching mid-session
- Rate limit detection from API responses

## Success Metrics
- Zero unexpected auth failures due to token expiry
- Users can identify problematic profiles at a glance
- Usage patterns are visible via dashboard
- TUI updates without manual refresh

## Design Document
See `docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md` for detailed design rationale, architecture decisions, and risk analysis.

## Sub-Epics
1. **Profile Health Foundation** - Core infrastructure
2. **Proactive Token Refresh** - Automatic token maintenance
3. **Usage Analytics** - Activity tracking and dashboard
4. **Runtime Enhancements** - Hot reload, signals
5. **Project Context** - Directory-profile associations

## Technical Approach
- SQLite for persistent storage (usage.db)
- JSON for lightweight metadata (health.json)
- Provider-specific OAuth handlers
- fsnotify for file watching","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:15:04.594984626-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T20:41:13.771976385-05:00","close_reason":"Planning complete: Design doc created at docs/SMART_PROFILE_MANAGEMENT.md. Implementation continues via sub-epics (caam-x0g, caam-e36, etc.)"}
 {"id":"caam-3b3","title":"Uninstall command","description":"Provide a clean way to remove caam and restore original state.

## Command
```
caam uninstall [flags]

Flags:
  --restore       Restore _original profiles before removing (default: true)
  --keep-vault    Keep vault directory (default: false)
  --keep-config   Keep config files (default: false)
  --force         Skip confirmation prompt
  --dry-run       Show what would be done without doing it
```

## Default Behavior (Interactive)
```
$ caam uninstall

caam uninstall will:
  ✓ Restore original auth for: claude, codex (gemini has no _original)
  ✓ Remove vault directory (~/.local/share/caam/vault/)
    - 3 claude profiles
    - 2 codex profiles  
  ✓ Remove config (~/.config/caam/)
  ✓ Remove activity database (~/.local/share/caam/caam.db)

Proceed? [y/N] y

Restoring claude auth from _original_claude... done
Restoring codex auth from _original_codex... done
Removing vault... done
Removing config... done
Removing database... done

caam has been uninstalled. Your original auth state has been restored.
```

## Edge Cases
- No _original exists for a tool → skip restore for that tool, warn user
- Vault doesn't exist → skip vault removal
- Restore fails → abort uninstall, report error

## Implementation
1. Create cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go
2. Add restore logic (iterate _original_* profiles)
3. Add cleanup logic (remove directories)
4. Add dry-run support
5. Add confirmation prompt

## Files
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go: New command
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall_test.go: Tests","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:28.458367802-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T23:19:41.414177099-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T23:19:41.414177099-05:00","close_reason":"Added caam uninstall: restores auth from vault/<tool>/_original before deleting caam artifacts; supports --dry-run/--keep-backups/--force; includes command tests.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"caam-3b3","depends_on_id":"caam-5fk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2025-12-17T21:45:39.688319849-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":44,"issue_id":"caam-3b3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Revised description - clarify operation order and fix profile naming:

## Purpose
Single command to completely reverse all caam changes and restore original state.
Critical for user trust: "I can always go back to how things were."

## Operation Order (CRITICAL)
The uninstall MUST happen in this exact order:

1. **RESTORE** original auth files from `_original` backup
2. **THEN** remove caam data (vault, config, database)

Why this order matters:
- If we delete first, we lose the backup needed to restore
- User would be left with whatever random profile was last active
- This violates the entire safety purpose

## Profile Naming Fix
Reference `_original` not `_original_claude` (see caam-5fk for rationale).

## Implementation

```bash
caam uninstall [--keep-backups] [--dry-run]
```

### Steps:
1. For each provider with an `_original` backup:
   a. Restore `_original` auth files to their canonical locations
   b. Verify restoration succeeded
2. If `--keep-backups` NOT set:
   a. Remove ~/.caam/vault/
   b. Remove ~/.caam/config/
   c. Remove ~/.caam/caam.db
3. Print summary of what was restored/removed

### Flags:
- `--keep-backups`: Restore originals but keep vault (user may want profiles later)
- `--dry-run`: Show what would happen without doing it
- `--force`: Skip confirmation prompt

## Files
- cmd/caam/cmd/uninstall.go: Main uninstall command
- internal/vault/restore.go: Restoration logic (separate from backup)

## Success Criteria
- Restores ALL original auth states before any deletion
- Single command, no manual steps required
- Dry-run shows exactly what will happen
- Works even if some providers have no _original (skips them with warning)
","created_at":"2025-12-18T03:06:35Z"},{"id":55,"issue_id":"caam-3b3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Claimed. Implementing  with restore-from  (per tool) before deleting caam data. Will include --dry-run/--keep-backups/--force and unit/integration tests.","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:09:25Z"},{"id":56,"issue_id":"caam-3b3","author":"ubuntu","text":"Implemented uninstall command: restores auth from vault/<tool>/_original before any deletion, then removes caam data/config/db/log/pid. Added flags --dry-run, --keep-backups, --force plus cmd tests for restore+cleanup, keep-backups, and dry-run.","created_at":"2025-12-18T04:18:56Z"}]}
 {"id":"caam-3ef","title":"Health status calculation logic","description":"# Health Status Calculation

## Purpose
Calculate profile health status (🟢🟡🔴) based on token expiry, errors, and penalties.

## Background (from codex-pool)
```go
score = headroom * planBonus * creditBonus - penalty
```

For caam v1, we simplify to status tiers with an internal numeric score.

## Technical Requirements

### Status Enum
```go
type HealthStatus int

const (
    StatusUnknown  HealthStatus = iota // ⚪ Cannot determine
    StatusHealthy                      // 🟢 Good to use
    StatusWarning                      // 🟡 Expiring soon or errors
    StatusCritical                     // 🔴 Expired or many errors
)

func (s HealthStatus) Icon() string
func (s HealthStatus) String() string
```

### Scoring Algorithm
```go
func CalculateHealth(h *ProfileHealth) (HealthStatus, float64) {
    score := 0.0
    now := time.Now()
    
    // Factor 1: Token expiry (primary)
    if h.TokenExpiresAt.IsZero() {
        // Unknown expiry - neutral
    } else if h.TokenExpiresAt.Before(now) {
        score -= 1.0  // Expired
    } else {
        ttl := h.TokenExpiresAt.Sub(now)
        switch {
        case ttl > time.Hour:
            score += 1.0
        case ttl > 15*time.Minute:
            score += 0.5
        default:
            // < 15 min: no bonus
        }
    }
    
    // Factor 2: Recent errors
    switch {
    case h.ErrorCount1h == 0:
        score += 0.3
    case h.ErrorCount1h <= 2:
        // Neutral
    default:
        score -= 0.5
    }
    
    // Factor 3: Plan type bonus
    switch h.PlanType {
    case "enterprise":
        score += 0.3
    case "pro", "team":
        score += 0.2
    }
    
    // Factor 4: Penalty (from errors, with decay)
    score -= h.Penalty
    
    // Convert to status
    status := StatusHealthy
    if score < 0 {
        status = StatusCritical
    } else if score < 0.5 {
        status = StatusWarning
    }
    
    return status, score
}
```

### Thresholds (Configurable)
```go
type HealthConfig struct {
    TokenExpiryWarningMinutes int     // Default: 60
    TokenExpiryCriticalMinutes int    // Default: 15
    ErrorCountWarning         int     // Default: 2
    ErrorCountCritical        int     // Default: 5
}
```

## User-Facing Display
```
🟢 Healthy   - Good to use
🟡 Warning   - Token expiring soon or recent errors
🔴 Critical  - Token expired or many errors
⚪ Unknown   - Cannot determine status
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] HealthStatus enum with Icon() and String()
- [ ] CalculateHealth function with documented algorithm
- [ ] Configurable thresholds via HealthConfig
- [ ] Unit tests covering all status transitions
- [ ] Edge cases: zero time, negative penalty, nil health

## Files to Create/Modify
- `internal/health/status.go` (new)
- `internal/health/status_test.go` (new)

## Dependencies
- Health metadata storage (caam-xxx)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2025-12-17T16:17:57.789256368-05:00","updated_at":"2025-12-17T17:14:39.883586788-05:00","closed_at":"2025-12-17T17:14:39.883586788-05:00","close_reason":"Implementation complete in status.go - HealthStatus enum, CalculateHealth function, HealthConfig, and tests all passing.
